disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Itch/burn with thick white clumpy 'cottage-cheese' discharge and NO fishy/foul odour, after antibiotics — candidiasis (not bacterial vaginosis's thin grey fishy-smelling discharge or trichomonas's frothy green-yellow discharge)
trigger_question_examples: Is the discharge thick and white/clumpy (cottage-cheese-like) with no fishy or foul smell, and is itch the main problem?
Did it start after a course of antibiotics?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it's thick, white and clumpy with no smell, the itch/burn is the worst part, and it started after my antibiotics.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No PID/upper-tract or STI features — no fever, no deep pelvic/lower-abdominal pain, no abnormal malodorous/frothy discharge, no intermenstrual/postcoital bleeding, no new-partner/STI-risk features suggesting a different infection
trigger_question_examples: Any fever, deep pelvic or lower-abdominal pain, or abnormal bleeding (which could suggest a deeper infection)?
Any new sexual partner or an offensive/frothy discharge (which points to a different infection)?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or deep pelvic pain, no abnormal bleeding, no new partner, and no offensive or frothy discharge.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pregnancy status to confirm (affects drug choice — avoid oral fluconazole in pregnancy, use topical), and recurrence pattern (>=4/year would prompt investigating an underlying cause e.g. diabetes)
trigger_question_examples: How many times a year do you get thrush — is it four or more?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't think I'm pregnant, and I only get it occasionally (not four times a year).
